evidence: Type model imports are split between `@/types/shots` and `@/types/generationAndShots` inside the same feature surface (`src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/VideoLightbox.tsx`, `src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx`, `src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts`), creating parallel dependency paths for the same domain entities.

`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts` and `src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx` depend directly on deep internals of `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox` (hooks barrel, inpainting types, provider/context, utility functions, and UI subcomponents), indicating boundary inversion where a tool feature is coupled to another feature’s internal structure.

Task-creation modules (`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/zImageTurboI2I.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`) each re-implement overlapping validation/default-building logic while depending on shared infra (`taskCreation`, `handleError`, LoRA types), which increases dependency surface and drift risk.

`src/shared/lib/supabaseSession.ts` contains auth-token key derivation/parsing (`getProjectRef`, `readRawSession`, `readUserIdFromStorage`) used by auth-dependent paths, but there is no dedicated `supabaseSession` unit test file under `src/shared/lib/__tests__`.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts` has a large orchestration path in `generateVideo()` (query wait, Supabase fetch, payload assembly, optional prompt clearing, task creation), while its test file focuses on helper exports (e.g. `_buildTravelRequestBody`, `_buildStructureGuidance`) and does not exercise `generateVideo()` end-to-end.

`src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts` listener lifecycle logic (`useAppEventListener`, `listenAppEvent`) and `src/shared/hooks/useLiveRelativeTimestamp.ts` timestamp update behavior are not directly unit-tested; current coverage is mostly indirect via consumer tests.
`src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts` instantiates `dataFreshnessManager` at module scope and mutates `window.__DATA_FRESHNESS_MANAGER__` during module evaluation in dev mode.
`src/app/routes.tsx` reads `import.meta.env` and builds a singleton `router` via `createBrowserRouter(...)` at import time, while also eagerly importing multiple tool pages.

`src/shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts` keeps module-level mutable singletons (`inflightSettingsFetches`, `cachedUser`, `cachedUserAt`) that persist across import order and require explicit reset hooks for isolation.

findings.summary: Generation domain types are sourced from two parallel modules, increasing dependency drift risk.
evidence: `useGenerationMutations`, `ImageLightbox`, and `VideoLightbox` import `GenerationRow` from `@/types/shots`.
`InlineEditView` and `generationTaskBridge` import `GenerationRow` from `@/types/generationAndShots`.
This creates two active dependency roots for the same entity in tightly related flows (gallery/lightbox/edit/mutations).
findings.suggestion: Complete the migration to a single canonical generation type module and update all imports in lightbox/edit/task bridge/mutation paths to that source; then remove or hard-fail legacy re-export usage to prevent reintroduction.

findings.summary: Edit-images feature is tightly coupled to MediaLightbox internals instead of a stable shared edit contract.
evidence: `useInlineEditState` imports many internals from `@/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks`, plus internal edit/inpainting types and utility functions.
`InlineEditView` imports `MediaDisplayWithCanvas`, controls, `EditModePanel`, `FloatingToolControls`, and `ImageEditProvider` directly from MediaLightbox internals.
The dependency direction ties edit-images evolution to MediaLightbox’s internal decomposition, so internal refactors in lightbox become cross-feature breaking changes.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared `image-edit-core` interface layer (state contract + reusable edit primitives) and have both MediaLightbox and edit-images consume that layer, keeping MediaLightbox layout internals private.

findings.summary: Task builder modules duplicate validation/default shaping, creating overlapping dependency stacks.
evidence: Each module defines local parameter interfaces and local validation flows while importing the same shared infra (`taskCreation`, `TaskValidationError`, `handleError`).
LoRA and seed/range validation logic appears in multiple task modules with slightly different representations (`PathLoraConfig`, `ComfyLoraConfig`, `FalLoraConfig`) and repeated hand-written checks.
Parallel per-task parameter builders increase change cost when shared constraints evolve (e.g., prompt/seed/range rules or common lineage fields).
findings.suggestion: Introduce shared task-schema utilities (common validators + normalized param builders) and let task-specific modules compose those schemas rather than re-implementing baseline checks and defaults.

findings.summary: Session token parsing/identity extraction is untested despite being auth-critical shared infrastructure.
evidence: `supabaseSession.ts` performs key auth behaviors (project-ref extraction from URL, JSON parsing from localStorage, token/user-id reads) that can silently return null on parse/env issues.
`toolSettingsService.ts` depends on `readUserIdFromStorage()` for lock-free auth resolution, so regressions in storage parsing can break settings fetch auth without explicit signal.
No dedicated `src/shared/lib/__tests__/supabaseSession.test.ts` exists; observed test references are mocks/indirect usage rather than contract-level assertions.
findings.suggestion: Add focused unit tests for `supabaseSession.ts` covering malformed URL, malformed JSON, missing token/user object, browser/non-browser guards, and key naming (`sb-<ref>-auth-token`) so downstream auth helpers fail predictably.

findings.summary: The main video-generation orchestration path lacks integration-style tests.
evidence: `generateVideo()` coordinates multiple critical steps (mutation wait, Supabase read, pair config generation, phase validation, task submission, conditional prompt clearing) and has multiple failure branches.
The service test suite validates many internal helper exports, but does not exercise `generateVideo()` flow with mocked Supabase/task creation and branch outcomes.
The request payload is cast to `TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams`, increasing risk of shape drift unless contract behavior is validated at the full-call boundary.
findings.suggestion: Add integration-style tests for `generateVideo()` that mock Supabase + `createTravelBetweenImagesTask` and assert full request shape and behavior across success, validation failure, fetch failure, and prompt-clear failure paths.

findings.summary: Core event and live-timestamp primitives are only indirectly covered, leaving lifecycle/timing regressions undetected.
evidence: `typedEvents.ts` contains direct `window` dispatch/listener wiring and React cleanup behavior, but coverage is mostly from consumers dispatching events, not direct primitive tests for subscribe/unsubscribe contracts.

`useLiveRelativeTimestamp.ts` handles date parsing, validity checks, and reactive formatting via `useTimestampUpdater`, yet there is no direct `useLiveRelativeTimestamp` test file under `src/shared/hooks/__tests__`.
Current `useUpdatingTimestamp` tests mock `useTimestampUpdater`, which bypasses real update-trigger behavior and can miss timing-related regressions in the shared live timestamp primitive.
findings.suggestion: Add direct tests for `typedEvents` listener cleanup/unsubscribe semantics and `useLiveRelativeTimestamp` behavior across invalid dates, disabled state, and update-trigger changes using fake timers.

findings.summary: Realtime freshness manager initializes globally at import and mutates window in module scope.
evidence: `DataFreshnessManager.ts` exports `const dataFreshnessManager = new DataFreshnessManager()` at module scope, creating process-wide state before any consumer opts in.
The same module writes `window.__DATA_FRESHNESS_MANAGER__ = dataFreshnessManager` at top level in dev, which is an import-time side effect.
`useTasks.ts` imports this singleton directly, so behavior is coupled to global instance lifecycle rather than hook-owned initialization.
findings.suggestion: Replace eager singleton export with a lazy accessor/factory (`getDataFreshnessManager`) and move dev window registration into an explicit bootstrap call invoked from app startup, not module evaluation.

findings.summary: Routing graph and environment branching are locked in at module load with eager feature imports.
evidence: `routes.tsx` computes `currentEnv` from `import.meta.env` at top level and immediately creates a module-scoped `router` via `createBrowserRouter(...)`.
The same module eagerly imports primary tool pages (including `JoinClipsPage`), forcing large feature modules to initialize whenever routes are imported.
Comments in `routes.tsx` already indicate prior initialization race behavior (hydration/Safari), confirming startup ordering sensitivity in this area.
findings.suggestion: Create the router inside a `createAppRouter()` function called from app bootstrap and convert more tool pages to guarded/lazy route elements where possible; keep env selection in bootstrap to reduce import-time coupling.

findings.summary: Tool settings service relies on module-level mutable caches shared across all callers.
evidence: `toolSettingsService.ts` defines module-global `inflightSettingsFetches`, `cachedUser`, and `cachedUserAt`, creating hidden shared state between unrelated consumers.
`setCachedUserId()` mutates that shared cache from external call sites, so behavior depends on whether another module has primed cache earlier in app lifetime.
Presence of `_resetCachedUserForTesting()` indicates tests must manually unwind global module state, a direct sign of initialization/lifecycle coupling.
findings.suggestion: Refactor to an injected service instance (per app/session/test) that owns cache and inflight maps, with consumers receiving the instance through context or explicit parameters instead of importing shared mutable module state.
